dropped: RISK-PBB-04 (gross impaired loans — 3rd PBB question, all-correct, redundant)
RISK-GENT-05 (gearing ratio — all-correct, replaced by higher-value GOV question)
FIN-SDG-03 (Sukuk terms — fails in all 3 paths, no comparative value)
SUS-YTLP-05 (ESG targets — fails in all 3 paths, no comparative value)
OPS-PADINI-02 (store count — trivially easy, PADINI already has easy + hard question)
added: FIN-CIMB-03 (CET1 + GIL — gives CIMB 2nd question; key risk metric)
GOV-GENT-03 (CEO remuneration — high layout; complex remuneration table)
FIN-NEST-04 (largest segment — high layout; segment note table)
FIN-BURSA-02 (trading breakdown — high layout; multi-column revenue table)
FIN-KPJ-Q3-02 (KPJ dividend tranches — high layout; B-solves/C-regresses discriminator)
OPS-YTLP-04 (Wessex Water RAB — high layout; replaces unanswerable ESG question)

questions.question: What was CIMB Group's net profit, ROE, and total income for FY2025, and how did these compare to FY2024?
questions.ground_truth: Net profit RM8.14bn (+14.6% YoY); ROE 12.4% (FY2024: 11.4%); total income grew strongly driven by NOII and Islamic banking. Forward4WARD target ROE >13% by 2027.

questions.question: What were CIMB Group's CET1 ratio and GIL ratio as at end-2025, and what was the YoY movement in each?
questions.ground_truth: CET1 ratio was 14.3%; GIL ratio improved to 1.7% (down 40bps YoY), the lowest ever recorded.

questions.question: What was Public Bank's net profit and total DPS for FY2025?
questions.ground_truth: Net profit RM7,224m; total DPS 22.5 sen

questions.question: What were Public Bank's CET1, Tier 1, Total Capital ratios and average LCR for FY2025?
questions.ground_truth: CET1 13.9%; Tier 1 13.9%; Total Capital 16.6%; avg LCR 127.7%

questions.question: What was Genting Malaysia's revenue and Adjusted EBITDA for FY2025, and by how much did each grow year-on-year?
questions.ground_truth: Revenue grew 9% YoY to RM11,884.3 million; Adjusted EBITDA grew 13% YoY to RM3,297.6 million (or 5% excluding FX translation effects).

questions.question: What was the total remuneration received by Tan Sri Lim Kok Thay as Deputy Chairman and CEO of Genting Malaysia for FY2025, and what was the breakdown by component?
questions.ground_truth: RM54,816,000 (Group basis): fee RM184k, allowance RM420k, salary RM35,585k, bonus RM8,392k, benefits-in-kind RM1,838k, other emoluments RM8,397k.

questions.question: What were Nestlé Group's total sales and net profit attributable to shareholders for FY2025, and what were the respective margins?
questions.ground_truth: Sales of CHF 89,490 million; net profit of CHF 9,033 million (10.1% net profit margin). Trading operating profit margin was 14.2%.

questions.question: Which was Nestlé's largest segment by sales in FY2025, and what were its sales revenue and trading operating profit?
questions.ground_truth: Zone Americas (AMS) was the largest segment with sales of CHF 34,482 million and trading operating profit of CHF 6,474 million.

questions.question: What were KPJ Healthcare's Group revenue and EBITDA for FY2025, and what milestones did these represent?
questions.ground_truth: Group revenue was RM4,258.4 million (+9% YoY), the first time exceeding RM4.0 billion. Group EBITDA was RM1.05 billion (+6% YoY), the first-ever RM1.0 billion EBITDA milestone.

questions.question: What was the total Board remuneration paid by KPJ Healthcare (Group) for FY2025, broken down by directorate category?
questions.ground_truth: Total Board remuneration was RM10,352k: Managing/Executive Director RM6,930k; Non-Independent Non-Executive Directors RM1,900k; Independent Non-Executive Directors RM1,522k.

questions.question: What were KPJ's revenue, EBITDA, PBT and PAT for the 9 months ended 30 September 2025 (continuing operations), and why does the YoY PAT comparison appear negative at total group level?
questions.ground_truth: Revenue RM3,106.9m (+9%); EBITDA RM734.8m (+7%); PBT RM380.3m (+9%); PAT to owners (continuing) RM233.1m (+8%). Total group PAT -4% (RM262.8m vs RM275.1m) because 9M FY2024 included RM32.1m discontinued ops profit vs near-nil in FY2025.

questions.question: What were the terms, properties, consideration and completion timeline of KPJ's sale-and-leaseback transaction with Al-'Aqar Healthcare REIT in 2025?
questions.ground_truth: APSH new building RM131m + PSH new building RM110m = RM241m total; SPAs announced 27 Mar 2025; unconditional 8 Jul 2025; 95% balance settled 7 Oct 2025; leases effective 7 Oct 2025. Four additional lease renewals commenced 1 Aug 2025 (PSH Main Building: 7 Oct 2025).

questions.question: What are all dividend tranches declared and paid for FY2025 as disclosed in the KPJ Q3 report, including the post-period declaration?
questions.ground_truth: 1st interim 1.05 sen (18 Apr, RM45.8m); special 0.10 sen (18 Apr, RM4.4m); 2nd interim 0.80 sen (11 Jul, RM34.9m); 3rd interim 1.05 sen (10 Oct, RM45.8m); 4th interim 1.23 sen approved 21 Nov (RM53.7m, payable 23 Dec 2025). YTD through Q3: 3.00 sen. Full-year cumulative: 4.23 sen.

questions.question: What was IHH Healthcare's GHG intensity in FY2025 vs the 2022 baseline?
questions.ground_truth: 146.0 kg/patient-bed-day (FY2025) vs 151.5 (2022); 8.2% reduction vs 2024

questions.question: What percentage of non-financial BSC targets were linked to sustainability at IHH, and what was the climate-specific proportion in 2025 vs 2026?
questions.ground_truth: ~80% non-financial targets linked to sustainability; climate ~5% in 2025, rising to 12.5% in 2026

questions.question: What was SD Guthrie's net profit for FY2025, its YoY change, and was it a record high?
questions.ground_truth: RM2,503m; +16% YoY from RM2,164m; record high

questions.question: What was SD Guthrie's SPOTT ranking and overall score in 2025?
questions.ground_truth: Ranked #1 globally (first time); 97.5% overall; 98.5% Community, Land & Labour Rights

questions.question: What were Bursa Malaysia's PATAMI and operating revenue for FY2025 vs FY2024?
questions.ground_truth: PATAMI RM250.2m (-19.3%); revenue RM701.8m (-7.4%)

questions.question: What was the breakdown of Bursa Malaysia's FY2025 operating revenue between trading and non-trading, and what were the major trading revenue components?
questions.ground_truth: Trading revenue RM445.2m (63.5%): Securities RM308.2m, Derivatives RM112.8m, BSAS RM20.2m; Non-trading revenue RM256.6m (36.5%), up 5.0% YoY.

questions.question: What were 99 Speed Mart's revenue, net profit and YoY growth rates for FY2025?
questions.ground_truth: Revenue RM11,433.2m (+14.5%); Net profit RM607.3m (+30.5%); GP RM2,226.5m; PBT RM803.4m

questions.question: How many total outlets did 99 Speed Mart operate at end FY2025, how many were added, and what is the 2026 target?
questions.ground_truth: 3,037 total (3,032 MY + 5 China); 259 added; target ~250 in 2026

questions.question: What was Oriental Kopi's segmental revenue breakdown for FY2025 and which was the fastest-growing segment?
questions.ground_truth: Café Chain RM418.6m (92.8%, +60.4%); Packaged Foods RM29.1m (6.5%, +153.0%); Others RM3.2m (0.7%)

questions.question: How much did Oriental Kopi raise in its IPO and what was the utilisation status as at 30 September 2025?
questions.ground_truth: RM184.0m raised; RM85.0m utilised (RM69.3m working capital, RM8.1m café expansion, RM7.6m listing); RM99.0m remaining

questions.question: What was TNB's revenue, PATAMI, DPS and dividend payout ratio in FY2025, and how did these compare to FY2024?
questions.ground_truth: Revenue RM67,723.1m (+19.4%); PATAMI RM4,768.1m (2024 restated: RM3,978.2m); DPS 53 sen (interim 25 sen + final 28 sen), highest ever; payout ratio 65.6% of adjusted PATAMI; dividend yield 3.86%.

questions.question: What were TNB's SAIDI for Peninsular Malaysia and Sabah, EAF and Transmission System Minutes in FY2025 vs FY2024?
questions.ground_truth: SAIDI Peninsular: 46.93 min (2024: 47.88); Sabah: 186.00 min (2024: 203.65); EAF: 87.8% (2024: 81.2%); Transmission Minutes: 0.15 min (2024: 0.0019 min)

questions.question: What is IBR Regulatory Period 4 (RP4) and what RE capacity milestones did TNB achieve or progress in FY2025?
questions.ground_truth: RP4 covers 2025-2027 under IBR framework; provides tariff certainty and investment clarity; RE milestones: 595 MW CRESS agreement signed; 102 MWp UK greenfield solar commercially operational (Jul 2025); 100 MW/400 MWh BESS Lahad Datu commissioned (Aug 2025); 300 MW Nenggiri Hydro 68% complete; total RE portfolio 4,681 MW (3,396 MY + 1,285 intl)

questions.question: What were the revenue and PBT contributions of each of YTL Power's four segments in FY2025, and which segment swung from a loss to a profit?
questions.ground_truth: Power Generation: RM12,190.5m / RM2,757.2m; Water & Sewerage: RM7,046.7m / RM384.3m (swung from loss RM101.0m — UK price increase + Ranhill consolidation); Telecoms: RM885.2m / loss RM256.0m (narrowed); Investment Holding: RM1,679.4m / RM424.9m

questions.question: What is Wessex Water's Regulatory Asset Base (RAB) value in FY2025, its capital investment plan for 2025–2030, and the key financing milestone achieved in March 2025?
questions.ground_truth: RAB £4.7bn/RM27.4bn (+9% from £4.3bn/RM25.8bn in FY2024); £3.65bn capital investment plan for 2025–2030 price review period; £600m sustainability bonds issued March 2025.

questions.question: What were Padini's revenue, PBT, gross profit margin and PAT for FY2025 vs FY2024?
questions.ground_truth: Revenue RM1,937.7m (+1.0%); PBT RM209.4m (+6.4%); GP margin 38.9% (FY2024: 36.2%); PAT RM154.8m (FY2024: RM146.6m). GP margin improved mainly due to stronger MYR reducing import COGS.

questions.question: What are all dividend tranches declared for FY2025 and the post-period FY2026 interim, and what was the payout ratio?
questions.ground_truth: 1st interim 2.50 sen; 2nd interim 2.50 sen; 3rd interim 1.80 sen; 4th interim 1.80 sen = total 9.60 sen DPS (FY2025). Post-period: 1st interim FY2026 of 1.80 sen declared 27 Aug 2025. Payout ratio 61.2%.
